options.description: interprets regular expressions as extended (modern) regular expressions rather than basic regular expressions
causes sed to delay opening each file until a command containing the related ``w'' function is applied to a line of input
appends the editing commands specified by the command argument to the list of commands
appends the editing commands found in the file command_file to the list of commands. The editing commands should each be listed on a separate line
edits files in-place, saving backups with the specified extension. If a zero-length extension is given, no backup will be saved
edits files in-place similarly to `-I`, but treats each file independently from other files. In particular, line numbers in each file start at 1, the ``$'' addr
by default, each line of input is echoed to the standard output after all of the commands have been applied to it. The `-n` option suppresses this behavior
same as `-E` for compatibility with GNU sed

subcommands.name: a
subcommands.description: appends `text` after a line

subcommands.name: b
subcommands.description: branch unconditionally to `label`

subcommands.name: c
subcommands.description: replace (change) lines with `text`

subcommands.name: d
subcommands.description: delete the pattern space; immediately start next cycle

subcommands.name: D
subcommands.description: if pattern space contains newlines, delete text in the pattern space up to the first newline, and restart cycle with the resultant pattern space, without readin

subcommands.name: e
subcommands.description: executes the command that is found in pattern space and replaces the pattern space with the output; a trailing newline is suppressed

subcommands.name: F
subcommands.description: prints the file name of the current input file

subcommands.name: g
subcommands.description: replaces the contents of the pattern space with the contents of the hold space

subcommands.name: G
subcommands.description: appends a newline to the contents of the pattern space, and then appends the contents of the hold space to that of the pattern space

subcommands.name: h
subcommands.description: replaces the contents of the hold space with the contents of the pattern space

subcommands.name: H
subcommands.description: appends a newline to the contents of the hold space, and then appends the contents of the pattern space to that of the hold space

subcommands.name: i
subcommands.description: insert text before a line

subcommands.name: l
subcommands.description: prints the pattern space in an unambiguous form

subcommands.name: n
subcommands.description: prints the pattern space, then, regardless, replaces the pattern space with the next line of input. If there is no more input then sed exits without processing

subcommands.name: N
subcommands.description: adds a newline to the pattern space, then appends the next line of input to the pattern space. If there is no more input then sed exits without processing any m

subcommands.name: p
subcommands.description: prints the pattern space

subcommands.name: P
subcommands.description: prints the pattern space up to the first newline

subcommands.name: q
subcommands.description: exit sed without processing any more commands or input

subcommands.name: Q
subcommands.description: this command is the same as q, but will not print the contents of pattern space

subcommands.name: r
subcommands.description: reads file

subcommands.name: R
subcommands.description: queue a line of filename to be read and inserted into the output stream at the end of the current cycle, or when the next input line is read

subcommands.name: s
subcommands.description: match the regular-expression against the content of the pattern space. If found, replace matched string with replacement

subcommands.name: t
subcommands.description: (test) Branch to label only if there has been a successful substitution since the last input line was read or conditional branch was taken. The label may be omi

subcommands.name: T
subcommands.description: (test) Branch to label only if there have been no successful substitutions since the last input line was read or conditional branch was taken. The label may be

subcommands.name: v
subcommands.description: makes sed fail if GNU sed extensions are not supported, or if the requested version is not available

subcommands.name: w
subcommands.description: writes the pattern space to the file

subcommands.name: W
subcommands.description: writes to the given filename the portion of the pattern space up to the first newline

subcommands.name: x
subcommands.description: exchanges the contents of the hold and pattern spaces

subcommands.name: y
subcommands.description: transliterate any characters in the pattern space which match any of the source-chars with the corresponding character in dest-chars

subcommands.name: z
subcommands.description: (zap) Empties the content of pattern space
comment until the next newline
